prompt:
Codex Mesh message dispatched from backend.

Message ID: msg_264231ef0b8d4610
From: Blog Outsider (blog-outsider)
To: Agent System (agent-system)
Type: request_context
Priority: high
Subject: Accelerate Tiger 2.0 Field College review loop

Body:
Context:\nBlog Outsider sent Tiger 2.0 a high priority Field College audit request: msg_6ac7d7d9ccac43e3. It was dispatched to Tiger thread 019e60d0-c588-7502-8db2-76b01cfeb7f2 and is currently pending as dispatch_0318e1e7af574c47.\n\nUser directive now:\nTalk to Arquitetura de agentes, verify whether I communicated correctly, accelerate, and in 15 minutes already be executing improvements after analysis. Be proactive, fast, autonomous, strategic.\n\nPlease evaluate:\n1. Did Blog Outsider use the mesh correctly for the Tiger 2.0 request?\n2. Is there a faster bridge path available from Agent System to actually send/read the Tiger thread reply?\n3. Should the pending dispatch be marked sent only after actual thread delivery, or is leaving it pending correct?\n4. Recommend the fastest robust loop for: Tiger feedback -> Blog Outsider receives -> implement Field College changes -> validate -> deploy.\n5. If you can help route the pending dispatch or expose an operational command, say exactly what command or handoff we should use.\n\nDo not edit files. Return concise operational guidance.

Expected output:
Short operational feedback: whether mesh usage was correct, how to accelerate Tiger review, exact next commands or bridge workflow, and any protocol correction.

Protocol:
- Preserve your project scope.
- Share only concise public context, decisions, interfaces, risks, and useful next steps.
- Do not edit files unless explicitly requested.
- If the answer should be shared back, keep it compact enough to write into the mesh backend.
